core_mechanics: Drawing cards triggers immediate, powerful, and random effects.
Deck size varies (13 or 22 cards); 9 cards are only present in the 22-card version.
Cards drawn in excess of declaration have no effect.
If draw limit is not met, remaining cards activate simultaneously.
Drawn cards (except Fool/Jester) fade and reappear later.

card_effects_summary.details: Lawful <-> Chaotic, Good <-> Evil. No effect on True Neutral/Unaligned.
Gain level's worth of XP if next hostile encounter is defeated single-handedly.
Entombed in an extradimensional sphere, immune to most divination. Draw stops.
-2 penalty to saving throws, ended only by a god or The Fates.
Avoid or erase one event as if it never happened. Usable at any time before death.
A powerful devil becomes a permanent enemy.
Lose 10,000 XP (or enough to prevent level loss) and draw again, counting both as one draw.
Gain 25 pieces of jewelry (2000 gp each) or 50 gems (1000 gp each).
Permanently reduce INT by 1d4+1 (min 1). Gain one extra draw.
Gain 10,000 XP OR draw two additional cards.
Gain a rare or rarer magic weapon chosen by the GM.
Gain service of a loyal 4th-level fighter (same race).
Gain 1d3 uses of the Wish spell.
A random NPC becomes hostile until removed by Wish or divine intervention.
All non-magic item wealth (portable property, real estate, documentation) is lost.
Summons an Avatar of Death that attacks the drawer. Helper summoning results in a new fight.
Increase one ability score by 2 (max 24).
Gain 50,000 XP and a random wondrous item.
All worn/carried magic items disintegrate (artifacts vanish).
Double Persuasion proficiency bonus and gain rightful ownership of a monster-held keep.
Gain the ability to ask one question and receive truthful, applicable wisdom within one year.
Soul is trapped in a guarded object; body becomes incapacitated. Draw stops.
Gain 10,000 XP OR draw two additional cards (not counting against the total draw limit if Joker drawn is not the Fool).

mechanics_analysis.notes: The mechanics are intentionally chaotic and high-variance, representing legendary, world-altering consequences. The structure defining card counts (13 vs 22) and the different outcomes for the two Jokers (Fool vs Jester) are clearly detailed, though the interaction between the Jester's bonus draws and the overall draw limit needs careful handling by the GM. Effects range from beneficial (Sun, Key, Moon) to catastrophic (Donjon, The Void, Skull).
